{"type":"system","subtype":"hook_started","hook_id":"ad897e1b-37fe-4e76-b3e1-ea9a7c241c1c","hook_name":"SessionStart:startup","hook_event":"SessionStart","uuid":"c9b9e39e-7313-4c64-af0e-2d0c4f9962cc","session_id":"1b038748-99c4-4824-ba50-f98226d592ea"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"hook_started","hook_id":"8610cf82-fb07-4811-92dd-b69343972430","hook_name":"SessionStart:startup","hook_event":"SessionStart","uuid":"c68d9191-789e-400a-83fc-9205307e764e","session_id":"1b038748-99c4-4824-ba50-f98226d592ea"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"hook_response","hook_id":"8610cf82-fb07-4811-92dd-b69343972430","hook_name":"SessionStart:startup","hook_event":"SessionStart","output":"{\"env\":{\"MINIMAX_COMPANION_SESSION_ID\":\"claude-1776861686035\"}}\n","stdout":"{\"env\":{\"MINIMAX_COMPANION_SESSION_ID\":\"claude-1776861686035\"}}\n","stderr":"","exit_code":0,"outcome":"success","uuid":"fc7f4e47-3683-4b66-8629-7ead760e9c0a","session_id":"1b038748-99c4-4824-ba50-f98226d592ea"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"hook_response","hook_id":"fa446fd5-daf8-4731-8dd9-d284ba2568a9","hook_name":"SessionStart:startup","hook_event":"SessionStart","output":"","stdout":"","stderr":"","exit_code":0,"outcome":"success","uuid":"1b93055b-d4e1-49a8-8c7a-11d19dd14985","session_id":"1b038748-99c4-4824-ba50-f98226d592ea"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"hook_response","hook_id":"ad897e1b-37fe-4e76-b3e1-ea9a7c241c1c","hook_name":"SessionStart:startup","hook_event":"SessionStart","output":"","stdout":"","stderr":"","exit_code":0,"outcome":"success","uuid":"0f18f13d-ef56-4364-a353-181fb370b6c5","session_id":"1b038748-99c4-4824-ba50-f98226d592ea"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"hook_response","hook_id":"7c194705-58d0-4938-aa96-f510e53cd1c2","hook_name":"SessionStart:startup","hook_event":"SessionStart","output":"","stdout":"","stderr":"","exit_code":0,"outcome":"success","uuid":"de2b88e1-fd42-4eee-b50b-c4567bdb1c60","session_id":"1b038748-99c4-4824-ba50-f98226d592ea"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"hook_response","hook_id":"68fd4691-0f0c-4f4f-bf6f-3e0e063e70fb","hook_name":"SessionStart:startup","hook_event":"SessionStart","output":"","stdout":"","stderr":"","exit_code":0,"outcome":"success","uuid":"9dc13ac7-371b-49bf-8968-a0e51087d8b5","session_id":"1b038748-99c4-4824-ba50-f98226d592ea"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"hook_response","hook_id":"af07f9de-0721-4384-8339-72d4128fd906","hook_name":"SessionStart:startup","hook_event":"SessionStart","output":"{\n  \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n    \"hookEventName\": \"SessionStart\",\n    \"additionalContext\": \"<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>\\nYou have superpowers.\\n\\n**Below is the full content of your 'superpowers:using-superpowers' skill - your introduction to using skills. For all other skills, use the 'Skill' tool:**\\n\\n---\\nname: using-superpowers\\ndescription: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions\\n---\\n\\n<SUBAGENT-STOP>\\nIf you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, skip this skill.\\n</SUBAGENT-STOP>\\n\\n<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>\\nIf you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply to what you are doing, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill.\\n\\nIF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.\\n\\nThis is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.\\n</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>\\n\\n## Instruction Priority\\n\\nSuperpowers skills override default system prompt behavior, but **user instructions always take precedence**:\\n\\n1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md, direct requests) — highest priority\\n2. **Superpowers skills** — override default system behavior where they conflict\\n3. **Default system prompt** — lowest priority\\n\\nIf CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, or AGENTS.md says \\\"don't use TDD\\\" and a skill says \\\"always use TDD,\\\" follow the user's instructions. The user is in control.\\n\\n## How to Access Skills\\n\\n**In Claude Code:** Use the `Skill` tool. When you invoke a skill, its content is loaded and presented to you—follow it directly. Never use the Read tool on skill files.\\n\\n**In Copilot CLI:** Use the `skill` tool. Skills are auto-discovered from installed plugins. The `skill` tool works the same as Claude Code's `Skill` tool.\\n\\n**In Gemini CLI:** Skills activate via the `activate_skill` tool. Gemini loads skill metadata at session start and activates the full content on demand.\\n\\n**In other environments:** Check your platform's documentation for how skills are loaded.\\n\\n## Platform Adaptation\\n\\nSkills use Claude Code tool names. Non-CC platforms: see `references/copilot-tools.md` (Copilot CLI), `references/codex-tools.md` (Codex) for tool equivalents. Gemini CLI users get the tool mapping loaded automatically via GEMINI.md.\\n\\n# Using Skills\\n\\n## The Rule\\n\\n**Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action.** Even a 1% chance a skill might apply means that you should invoke the skill to check. If an invoked skill turns out to be wrong for the situation, you don't need to use it.\\n\\n```dot\\ndigraph skill_flow {\\n    \\\"User message received\\\" [shape=doublecircle];\\n    \\\"About to EnterPlanMode?\\\" [shape=doublecircle];\\n    \\\"Already brainstormed?\\\" [shape=diamond];\\n    \\\"Invoke brainstorming skill\\\" [shape=box];\\n    \\\"Might any skill apply?\\\" [shape=diamond];\\n    \\\"Invoke Skill tool\\\" [shape=box];\\n    \\\"Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'\\\" [shape=box];\\n    \\\"Has checklist?\\\" [shape=diamond];\\n    \\\"Create TodoWrite todo per item\\\" [shape=box];\\n    \\\"Follow skill exactly\\\" [shape=box];\\n    \\\"Respond (including clarifications)\\\" [shape=doublecircle];\\n\\n    \\\"About to EnterPlanMode?\\\" -> \\\"Already brainstormed?\\\";\\n    \\\"Already brainstormed?\\\" -> \\\"Invoke brainstorming skill\\\" [label=\\\"no\\\"];\\n    \\\"Already brainstormed?\\\" -> \\\"Might any skill apply?\\\" [label=\\\"yes\\\"];\\n    \\\"Invoke brainstorming skill\\\" -> \\\"Might any skill apply?\\\";\\n\\n    \\\"User message received\\\" -> \\\"Might any skill apply?\\\";\\n    \\\"Might any skill apply?\\\" -> \\\"Invoke Skill tool\\\" [label=\\\"yes, even 1%\\\"];\\n    \\\"Might any skill apply?\\\" -> \\\"Respond (including clarifications)\\\" [label=\\\"definitely not\\\"];\\n    \\\"Invoke Skill tool\\\" -> \\\"Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'\\\";\\n    \\\"Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'\\\" -> \\\"Has checklist?\\\";\\n    \\\"Has checklist?\\\" -> \\\"Create TodoWrite todo per item\\\" [label=\\\"yes\\\"];\\n    \\\"Has checklist?\\\" -> \\\"Follow skill exactly\\\" [label=\\\"no\\\"];\\n    \\\"Create TodoWrite todo per item\\\" -> \\\"Follow skill exactly\\\";\\n}\\n```\\n\\n## Red Flags\\n\\nThese thoughts mean STOP—you're rationalizing:\\n\\n| Thought | Reality |\\n|---------|---------|\\n| \\\"This is just a simple question\\\" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |\\n| \\\"I need more context first\\\" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |\\n| \\\"Let me explore the codebase first\\\" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |\\n| \\\"I can check git/files quickly\\\" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |\\n| \\\"Let me gather information first\\\" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |\\n| \\\"This doesn't need a formal skill\\\" | If a skill exists, use it. |\\n| \\\"I remember this skill\\\" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |\\n| \\\"This doesn't count as a task\\\" | Action = task. Check for skills. |\\n| \\\"The skill is overkill\\\" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |\\n| \\\"I'll just do this one thing first\\\" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |\\n| \\\"This feels productive\\\" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |\\n| \\\"I know what that means\\\" | Knowing the concept ≠ using the skill. Invoke it. |\\n\\n## Skill Priority\\n\\nWhen multiple skills could apply, use this order:\\n\\n1. **Process skills first** (brainstorming, debugging) - these determine HOW to approach the task\\n2. **Implementation skills second** (frontend-design, mcp-builder) - these guide execution\\n\\n\\\"Let's build X\\\" → brainstorming first, then implementation skills.\\n\\\"Fix this bug\\\" → debugging first, then domain-specific skills.\\n\\n## Skill Types\\n\\n**Rigid** (TDD, debugging): Follow exactly. Don't adapt away discipline.\\n\\n**Flexible** (patterns): Adapt principles to context.\\n\\nThe skill itself tells you which.\\n\\n## User Instructions\\n\\nInstructions say WHAT, not HOW. \\\"Add X\\\" or \\\"Fix Y\\\" doesn't mean skip workflows.\\n\\n\\n</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>\"\n  }\n}\n","stdout":"{\n  \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n    \"hookEventName\": \"SessionStart\",\n    \"additionalContext\": \"<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>\\nYou have superpowers.\\n\\n**Below is the full content of your 'superpowers:using-superpowers' skill - your introduction to using skills. For all other skills, use the 'Skill' tool:**\\n\\n---\\nname: using-superpowers\\ndescription: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions\\n---\\n\\n<SUBAGENT-STOP>\\nIf you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, skip this skill.\\n</SUBAGENT-STOP>\\n\\n<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>\\nIf you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply to what you are doing, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill.\\n\\nIF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.\\n\\nThis is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.\\n</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>\\n\\n## Instruction Priority\\n\\nSuperpowers skills override default system prompt behavior, but **user instructions always take precedence**:\\n\\n1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md, direct requests) — highest priority\\n2. **Superpowers skills** — override default system behavior where they conflict\\n3. **Default system prompt** — lowest priority\\n\\nIf CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, or AGENTS.md says \\\"don't use TDD\\\" and a skill says \\\"always use TDD,\\\" follow the user's instructions. The user is in control.\\n\\n## How to Access Skills\\n\\n**In Claude Code:** Use the `Skill` tool. When you invoke a skill, its content is loaded and presented to you—follow it directly. Never use the Read tool on skill files.\\n\\n**In Copilot CLI:** Use the `skill` tool. Skills are auto-discovered from installed plugins. The `skill` tool works the same as Claude Code's `Skill` tool.\\n\\n**In Gemini CLI:** Skills activate via the `activate_skill` tool. Gemini loads skill metadata at session start and activates the full content on demand.\\n\\n**In other environments:** Check your platform's documentation for how skills are loaded.\\n\\n## Platform Adaptation\\n\\nSkills use Claude Code tool names. Non-CC platforms: see `references/copilot-tools.md` (Copilot CLI), `references/codex-tools.md` (Codex) for tool equivalents. Gemini CLI users get the tool mapping loaded automatically via GEMINI.md.\\n\\n# Using Skills\\n\\n## The Rule\\n\\n**Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action.** Even a 1% chance a skill might apply means that you should invoke the skill to check. If an invoked skill turns out to be wrong for the situation, you don't need to use it.\\n\\n```dot\\ndigraph skill_flow {\\n    \\\"User message received\\\" [shape=doublecircle];\\n    \\\"About to EnterPlanMode?\\\" [shape=doublecircle];\\n    \\\"Already brainstormed?\\\" [shape=diamond];\\n    \\\"Invoke brainstorming skill\\\" [shape=box];\\n    \\\"Might any skill apply?\\\" [shape=diamond];\\n    \\\"Invoke Skill tool\\\" [shape=box];\\n    \\\"Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'\\\" [shape=box];\\n    \\\"Has checklist?\\\" [shape=diamond];\\n    \\\"Create TodoWrite todo per item\\\" [shape=box];\\n    \\\"Follow skill exactly\\\" [shape=box];\\n    \\\"Respond (including clarifications)\\\" [shape=doublecircle];\\n\\n    \\\"About to EnterPlanMode?\\\" -> \\\"Already brainstormed?\\\";\\n    \\\"Already brainstormed?\\\" -> \\\"Invoke brainstorming skill\\\" [label=\\\"no\\\"];\\n    \\\"Already brainstormed?\\\" -> \\\"Might any skill apply?\\\" [label=\\\"yes\\\"];\\n    \\\"Invoke brainstorming skill\\\" -> \\\"Might any skill apply?\\\";\\n\\n    \\\"User message received\\\" -> \\\"Might any skill apply?\\\";\\n    \\\"Might any skill apply?\\\" -> \\\"Invoke Skill tool\\\" [label=\\\"yes, even 1%\\\"];\\n    \\\"Might any skill apply?\\\" -> \\\"Respond (including clarifications)\\\" [label=\\\"definitely not\\\"];\\n    \\\"Invoke Skill tool\\\" -> \\\"Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'\\\";\\n    \\\"Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'\\\" -> \\\"Has checklist?\\\";\\n    \\\"Has checklist?\\\" -> \\\"Create TodoWrite todo per item\\\" [label=\\\"yes\\\"];\\n    \\\"Has checklist?\\\" -> \\\"Follow skill exactly\\\" [label=\\\"no\\\"];\\n    \\\"Create TodoWrite todo per item\\\" -> \\\"Follow skill exactly\\\";\\n}\\n```\\n\\n## Red Flags\\n\\nThese thoughts mean STOP—you're rationalizing:\\n\\n| Thought | Reality |\\n|---------|---------|\\n| \\\"This is just a simple question\\\" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |\\n| \\\"I need more context first\\\" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |\\n| \\\"Let me explore the codebase first\\\" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |\\n| \\\"I can check git/files quickly\\\" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |\\n| \\\"Let me gather information first\\\" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |\\n| \\\"This doesn't need a formal skill\\\" | If a skill exists, use it. |\\n| \\\"I remember this skill\\\" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |\\n| \\\"This doesn't count as a task\\\" | Action = task. Check for skills. |\\n| \\\"The skill is overkill\\\" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |\\n| \\\"I'll just do this one thing first\\\" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |\\n| \\\"This feels productive\\\" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |\\n| \\\"I know what that means\\\" | Knowing the concept ≠ using the skill. Invoke it. |\\n\\n## Skill Priority\\n\\nWhen multiple skills could apply, use this order:\\n\\n1. **Process skills first** (brainstorming, debugging) - these determine HOW to approach the task\\n2. **Implementation skills second** (frontend-design, mcp-builder) - these guide execution\\n\\n\\\"Let's build X\\\" → brainstorming first, then implementation skills.\\n\\\"Fix this bug\\\" → debugging first, then domain-specific skills.\\n\\n## Skill Types\\n\\n**Rigid** (TDD, debugging): Follow exactly. Don't adapt away discipline.\\n\\n**Flexible** (patterns): Adapt principles to context.\\n\\nThe skill itself tells you which.\\n\\n## User Instructions\\n\\nInstructions say WHAT, not HOW. \\\"Add X\\\" or \\\"Fix Y\\\" doesn't mean skip workflows.\\n\\n\\n</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>\"\n  }\n}\n","stderr":"","exit_code":0,"outcome":"success","uuid":"e5ef5dbe-4149-48e6-a3dc-f71a8db8048a","session_id":"1b038748-99c4-4824-ba50-f98226d592ea"}

ig","mcp__engram__list_sessions","mcp__engram__live_sessions","mcp__engram__manage_project_alias","mcp__engram__project_archive","mcp__engram__project_list_migrations","mcp__engram__project_move","mcp__engram__project_move_batch","mcp__engram__project_recover","mcp__engram__project_review","mcp__engram__project_timeline","mcp__engram__project_undo","mcp__engram__save_insight","mcp__engram__search","mcp__engram__stats","mcp__engram__tool_analytics","mcp__plugin_context7_context7__query-docs","mcp__plugin_context7_context7__resolve-library-id"],"mcp_servers":[{"name":"plugin:context7:context7","status":"connected"},{"name":"pencil","status":"failed"},{"name":"MCP_DOCKER","status":"failed"},{"name":"engram","status":"connected"},{"name":"chrome-devtools","status":"connected"},{"name":"claude.ai dex:codex-rescue","Explore","gemini:gemini-agent","general-purpose","kimi:kimi-agent","minimax:minimax-agent","Plan","qwen:qwen-rescue","statusline-setup","superpowers:code-reviewer"],"skills":["update-config","debug","simplify","batch","fewer-permission-prompts","loop","schedule","claude-api","agent-browser","using-superpowers","surge","find-skills","fireworks-tech-graph","codex:result","codex:adversarial-review","codex:status","codex:cancel","codex:review","gemini:status","gemini:adversarial-review","gemini:result","gemini:timing","gemini:cancel","gemini:review","kimi:result","kimi:adversarial-review","kimi:status","kimi:cancel","kimi:review","qwen:adversarial-review","qwen:result","qwen:ask","qwen:cancel","qwen:status","qwen:review","superpowers:receiving-code-review","superpowers:writing-plans","superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch","superpowers:requesting-code-review","superpowers:executing-plans","superpowers:subagent-driven-development","superpowers:systematic-debugging","superpowers:verification-before-completion","superpowers:brainstorming","superpowers:using-superpowers","superpowers:using-git-worktrees","superpowers:writing-skills","superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents","superpowers:test-driven-development","kimi:kimi-prompting","kimi:kimi-result-handling","kimi:kimi-cli-runtime","minimax:minimax-prompting","minimax:minimax-cli-runtime","minimax:minimax-result-handling"],"plugins":[{"name":"context7","path":"/home/user/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/context7/unknown","source":"context7@claude-plugins-official"},{"name":"swift-lsp","path":"/home/user/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/swift-lsp/1.0.0","source":"swift-lsp@claude-plugins-official"},{"name":"claude-hud","path":"/home/user/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-hud/claude-hud/0.0.12","source":"claude-hud@claude-hud"},{"name":"codex","path":"/home/user/.claude/plugins/cache/openai-codex/codex/1.0.4","source":"codex@openai-codex"},{"name":"superpowers","path":"/home/user
